claim_text: LPIPS uses an L2-norm for G, VGG-16^46 trained on ImageNet for φ_D, and additionally learns a linear combination of DFDs extracted from five different convolutional layers l based on perceptual judgment scores.

sub_claims.text: VGG-16 is a 16-weight-layer convolutional network architecture (Configuration D) introduced by Simonyan & Zisserman (2014/2015), trained on ImageNet (ILSVRC-2012).

evidence.snippet: a significant improvement on the prior-art configurations can be achieved by pushing the depth to 16–19 weight layers. These findings were the basis of our ImageNet Challenge 2014 submission
we use the ConvNet architecture D (Table 1), which contains 16 weight layers and was found to be the best-performing in the classification task
We evaluated the described ConvNet architectures on the ILSVRC-2012 dataset (which was used for ILSVRC 2012–2014 challenges). The dataset includes images of 1000 classes
the classification error decreases with the increased ConvNet depth: from 11 layers in A to 19 layers in E ... the configuration D, which uses 3 × 3 conv.

sub_claims.nuance: The paper does not use the literal label 'VGG-16'; it refers to the network as 'configuration D' / '16 weight layers' and team/model name 'VGG' (Net-D). 'VGG-16' is the universally adopted community shorthand for exactly this 16-weight-layer Configuration D, and the paper also confirms ImageNet (ILSVRC-2012) training.

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Simonyan & Zisserman (2014/2015) is the primary source for the VGG family of ConvNets; the 16-weight-layer Configuration D is introduced in this paper and no other primary is credited for it.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript cites simonyan2014 strictly as the origin of the VGG-16 backbone used by LPIPS as a feature extractor φ_D, pretrained on ImageNet. The source paper introduces exactly that architecture and pretrains it on ILSVRC-2012 ImageNet, which is the identical usage context (ImageNet-pretrained feature backbone). No context mismatch. The downstream 'LPIPS uses ...' claim is grounded against the LPIPS paper on its own claim, not here.
